title: Telegram smoke checklist + mocked handler tests (the T-244..T-248 cluster shipped blind)
current_state: The T-244..T-248 Telegram bug cluster (context loss, quote-reply miss, literal-empty message, HTML 400, empty-photo IndexError) all shipped as bugs-in-production and were only caught when Ash hit them live. Handler-level coverage is effectively zero: existing tests mock below the handler layer, and there is no documented manual smoke procedure to run after touching tools/tools_telegram.py.
target_state: (1) docs/TELEGRAM_SMOKE.md: a 5-step manual checklist to run after any telegram change - plain text message, photo with caption, quote-reply to an old message, inline-button tap, long/HTML-heavy response (>4096 chars). (2) 2-3 mocked handler tests in testing/ exercising real handler code with a fake bot object: send-path formatting fallback (malformed HTML degrades to stripped plain text, not a 400 crash) and callback routing (tap reaches _process_text).
migration_plan: 1. Write docs/TELEGRAM_SMOKE.md with the 5 steps above, each with its expected outcome, referencing which past ticket each step would have caught (T-246 empty, T-247 HTML, T-248 photo, T-245 quote, T-220 buttons).
2. Add testing/test_telegram_handlers.py: fake bot class capturing send_message/send_photo calls; drive the real handler functions from tools/tools_telegram.py.
3. Test A: response containing unbalanced HTML -> handler falls back to stripped plain text, fake bot receives a message (regression guard for T-247's fix).
4. Test B: callback update -> routed into _process_text with the payload string (regression guard for the T-220 path that T-258 will build on).
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS - the new tests join the non-costly set (no real Telegram API calls).
risk_notes: Component is testing/ + docs/ only - fully inside SAFE_COMPONENTS with a reproducing-test shape, making this the ideal first candidate for T-256's sprint production run. Keep the fake bot minimal (a class with 3 methods), not a python-telegram-bot test framework import.
